title: Localize 4 board-flagged pages: company access, user board, instance general, instance adapters (parent ZAI-175)
description:
Parent: [ZAI-175](/SDF/issues/ZAI-175) — board flagged untranslated content on these four pages while viewing the Russian locale.

IMPORTANT: Per ZAI-163/ZAI-164, verify cwd is `paperclip_fork_Enterprise` (the fork) before committing. Do NOT leak i18n into the upstream public repo. The current branch is `vib-1171-2652-2760-3582-localization` — keep work on that branch.

## Pre-flight check before duplicating effort

Before starting, confirm none of this scope overlaps with already-in-flight work:
- ZAI-159 covers `/instance/settings/access` (a DIFFERENT page from `/SDF/company/settings/access`).
- ZAI-160 covers `/instance/settings/experimental` (different).
- ZAI-170 covers `/SDF/org`, `/SDF/skills/{id}`, `/SDF/costs`, `/SDF/company/settings/invites` (distinct from `/access`).
- ZAI-174 is the upstream-sync findings doc (not these pages).

If any of the strings below are already wired through `t()` on master or on the current branch, just confirm the keys exist in all 8 locales and skip. Don't duplicate.

## Page 1 — `/SDF/company/settings/access`

Board-confirmed leaks (from ZAI-175 description and `qa-dom-leaks.json`, present in ru and de):

- The `Remove` button label inside member rows (svg icon + `Remove` text, selector `div.flex > button.inline-flex.items-center[title]`).
- Helper text `You cannot remove yourself.` (selector `div.space-y-1 > div.text-xs.text-muted-foreground`).

**Server-side string** — `You cannot remove yourself.` originates in `server/src/routes/access.ts:1095` and `server/src/services/access.ts:466`. The frontend currently surfaces this raw English text. Two options:
  1. Have the server return a stable error code (e.g. `cannot_remove_self`), and translate on the frontend. Preferred — consistent with the rest of the i18n approach.
  2. Make the server locale-aware. Heavier; needs request-locale plumbing.

Decision is yours; document the choice in the PR. If you go with option 1, check whether other access-route errors already use a code-based pattern and follow precedent.

Trigger condition for the helper text: it shows on your own row to disable self-removal. The Remove button shows on rows belonging to other members.

## Page 2 — `/SDF/u/board` (user profile)

The board flagged the meta line `owner · active · присоединился May 5, 2026` — note that `присоединился` IS already translated, but the surrounding tokens (`owner`, `active`) are NOT, and they sit next to a translated word, which looks worse than fully-English would. Source: `ui/src/pages/UserProfile.tsx:262-266`:
```
const metaParts = [
  data.user.membershipRole ?? "member",   // "owner" / "admin" / "member"
  data.user.membershipStatus,             // "active" / "invited" / "suspended"
  t("user_profile.joined", { date: formatDate(data.user.joinedAt) }),
];
```
`membershipRole` and `membershipStatus` are raw enum values from the DB. Add `user_profile.role.{owner,admin,member}` and `user_profile.status.{active,invited,suspended,...}` translation keys (check `server/src/db/schema/*.ts` or wherever the enum is defined for the full set), and translate before joining.

Also flagged: section headings rendered by `WindowColumn` (`UserProfile.tsx:60`):
- `Last 7 days`
- `Last 30 days`
- `All time`

These come from the server response (`stats[].label`). Source: `server/src/routes/user-profiles.ts:34-38`:
```
const PROFILE_WINDOWS = [
  { key: "last7", label: "Last 7 days", days: 7 },
  { key: "last30", label: "Last 30 days", days: 30 },
  { key: "all", label: "All time", days: null },
] as const;
```
Same architectural choice as Page 1. Cleanest fix: stop sending `label` from the server, send only `key`, and translate on the frontend (`user_profile.window.{last7,last30,all}`). Keep `key` for stable routing/sorting.

## Page 3 — `/instance/settings/general`

File: `ui/src/pages/InstanceGeneralSettings.tsx`. Currently has ZERO `useTranslation`/`t()` calls — the entire page is hardcoded English. Board-flagged samples:
- Breadcrumb `Instance Settings` (rendered twice per board screenshot)
- Section heading `Deployment and auth`

Full-page sweep required: every visible string → `t()` + keys in all 8 locales (`en`, `ru`, `de`, `el`, `es`, `pt`, `uk`, `zh`). Locale namespace TBD by you — `settings` already exists and contains `Deployment and auth` in en, so prefer extending that namespace.

## Page 4 — `/instance/settings/adapters`

File: `ui/src/pages/AdapterManager.tsx`. Has 4 `t()` calls but board says "вся страница НЕ переведена" — partial. Full sweep required: identify remaining hardcoded strings, wire through `t()`, add keys to all 8 locales.

## Locales

All 8: `en`, `ru`, `de`, `el`, `es`, `pt`, `uk`, `zh`. Use the existing translation patterns already on the branch (consult prior i18n PRs landed under this branch for tone/terminology).

## Acceptance criteria

1. Visiting each of the four pages with `?locale=ru` (or any of the other 7 locales) shows zero hardcoded English strings in any visible UI element.
2. The DOM walker (`qa-dom-walk.mjs` or whatever the current sweep harness is — check ZAI-151 for the latest invocation pattern) reports zero leaks for these four pages across all 8 locales.
3. No regressions on existing localized pages.
4. Server-side decisions (whether `Last 7 days` and `You cannot remove yourself.` are translated server-side or via key passthrough) are documented in the PR description.
5. cwd verified as fork before any commit. No changes leak to the upstream public repo.

## How to verify

- Boot the local instance, log in, switch UI to `ru` (and at minimum one more non-en locale), and walk all four pages manually. Screenshot any remaining English.
- Run a targeted DOM sweep against the four URLs across all 8 locales. Attach the report.
- Per memory `feedback_quote_qa_verdict_verbatim`: when this comes back for review, the assignee MUST quote the QA verdict line verbatim — do not paraphrase.

## Out of scope

- The other localization rounds in flight (ZAI-159, ZAI-160, ZAI-170, ZAI-139). Do not touch those pages here.
- Backend route restructuring beyond what the i18n decision requires.
- The upstream-sync findings doc (ZAI-174 owns that).

## When done

Move to `in_review` and assign back to CEO (`db69a3af-4281-40f9-9612-00d9c9c80315`). The board (human) reviews via my approval; do not auto-merge the localization branch.
